name: Wisconsin Audiologist License
description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Wisconsin - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Examining Board of Speech-Language Pathologists and Audiologists
provider.parentOrganization: Wisconsin Department of Safety and Professional Services (DSPS)

address.description:
Wisconsin Department of Safety and Professional Services
P.O. Box 8935
Madison, WI 53708-8935

requiredDocuments.name: Official Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Sent directly from your educational institution.

requiredDocuments.name: Exam Score Report
requiredDocuments.description: Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Wisconsin audiologist license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.
Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

mainEntity.name: Does Wisconsin have reciprocity for audiologists?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Wisconsin participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Wisconsin without a separate endorsement application.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to Wisconsin?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Wisconsin while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
